title: Pattern Aggregation Ledger v1
description:
Validates a single entry in the high-frequency append-only pattern-aggregation ledger at `<project_root>/.claude/brain/pattern-aggregation-ledger.jsonl`. The ledger captures TWO row kinds (per E-B2-7 Q4 oneOf discrimination): (a) `ReceivedEntry` rows written by the federated-pattern default-receive handler — capturing every incoming federated-pattern envelope (including drops with their `dropped_reason`); and (b) `EmittedEntry` rows written by the federated-pattern sender wiring — capturing every outgoing federated-pattern envelope BEFORE transmission (operator audit trail per Q12). This is the FIRST Brains-2.0 ledger that captures BOTH inbound and outbound rows. The ledger remains gitignored runtime state; v1 is per-Brain LOCAL only with no A2A propagation.

Discriminator: `entry_kind ∈ {received, emitted}`. `received` rows add `from_brain_id` + `envelope_message_id` + optional `dropped_reason`; `emitted` rows add `to_brain_id` + `envelope_message_id`. Both kinds carry `schema_version` (const `1`), `entry_kind`, `ts` (ISO 8601 UTC), `peer_brain_id` (opaque hash), and `payload` (the full federated-pattern payload as an opaque object — the schema validates ledger STRUCTURE; the federated-pattern schema validates payload CONTENT at the message-handler layer). Cross-schema $ref to `a2a-federated-pattern-v1.schema.json` is intentionally avoided at the ledger schema layer to dodge cross-file resolution complexity.

Privacy contract: operator handle is NOT captured (federation is project-level, not operator-level — distinct from `domain-calibration-ledger-v1.TriagedEntry.human_operator` and `invocation-ledger-v1.DispositionEntry.human_operator`). The closed-set `DroppedReason` enum is the v1 vocabulary lock (Q4 + Q6 + Q11 + Q15) — new vocabulary terms require a spec change with an explicit METHODOLOGY-EVOLUTION entry. Per-entry forward-compat is via the optional `extensions` block (E4-7 pattern, mirrors `invocation-ledger-v1.SkillEntry.extensions`); top-level schema validates ONE row at a time. Closed-set discipline on `DroppedReason` is the v1 vocabulary lock.

DroppedReason.description: Closed-vocabulary drop-reason enum (Q4 + Q6 + Q11 + Q15). Receiver-side reasons for dropping an inbound federated-pattern. `rate-limit-exceeded` = peer exceeded sliding-window per-peer threshold (Q6, BR-6 mitigation); `recursion-guard` = receiver's own opaque-hash was in `origin_set[]` (Q9 wire-level loop closure); `schema-validation-failed` = payload failed federated-pattern schema validation (Q3); `hop-limit-exceeded` = `origin_set.length > MAX_HOPS=4` (Q15 hop limit); `unknown-pattern-kind` = receiver does not understand the value of `pattern_kind` (Q11 cross-version compat — graceful degradation). Closed-set v1 (Q4 lock); new vocabulary terms require a spec change with an explicit METHODOLOGY-EVOLUTION entry — same additive discipline as `invocation-ledger-v1.Disposition`, `hat-contract-v1.ToolName`, `trust-budget-v1.Ecosystem`. Vocabulary changes surface as deliberate test edits, not silent drift.

ReceivedEntry.description: A federated-pattern receipt row written by the default-receive handler (`federated_pattern.rs` — landing at C4). Captures the full inbound envelope including drop information when applicable. Drops are recorded as a row with `dropped_reason` populated; accepted messages have no `dropped_reason`. Either way every receipt is logged for operator visibility (Q3 receiver disposition + Q6 BR-6 observability).

schema_version.description: Pattern-aggregation-ledger schema version. Forward-compat per E4-7: any v2 schema (additive per charter discipline #2) requires bumping this const so v1 consumers detect the version mismatch deterministically.

entry_kind.description: Row-kind discriminator for `ReceivedEntry`. Distinguishes from `EmittedEntry` (which uses `entry_kind: "emitted"`).

ts.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at receive time. Monotonically increasing within a single ledger; uniqueness is the writer's responsibility.

peer_brain_id.description: Opaque-hash identifier of the local Brain (the receiver — i.e., the Brain that owns this ledger). Same opaque-hash format as `a2a-federated-pattern-v1.peer_brain_id`.

from_brain_id.description: Opaque-hash identifier of the sending Brain. Same opaque-hash format as `a2a-federated-pattern-v1.anonymized_origin`.

envelope_message_id.description: The `message_id` of the enclosing `a2a-envelope-v1`. Allows operators to correlate ledger rows with on-the-wire envelope traces.

payload.description: The full federated-pattern payload as received. The schema validates ledger STRUCTURE; the federated-pattern schema validates payload CONTENT at the message-handler layer (NOT here). Cross-schema $ref to `a2a-federated-pattern-v1.schema.json` is intentionally avoided to dodge cross-file resolution complexity. Field-level validation runs at the federated-pattern handler before this row is appended.

dropped_reason.description: OPTIONAL. Populated for receiver-side drops (rate-limit, recursion-guard, schema-validation-failed, hop-limit-exceeded, unknown-pattern-kind). Absent or null for accepted messages. Closed-set 5-entry vocabulary per Q4.

extensions.description: E4-7 forward-compat surface. Operators MAY add experimental fields under this object without a schema bump — explicit experimentation space, mirroring `invocation-ledger-v1.SkillEntry.extensions`. Fields under `extensions` are NOT normative; the sensor MUST ignore them at v1; v2 may promote individual extensions to top-level fields with the standard additive-promotion discipline.

EmittedEntry.description: A federated-pattern emission row written by the sender wiring BEFORE transmission (Q12 operator audit trail). Every emit is logged in the SENDER'S OWN ledger with `to_brain_id`, `envelope_message_id`, `payload`, `ts`. Operator MAY review what their Brain has shared via `neurogrim sensory federated-patterns`. NEUROGRIM_OPERATOR identity is NOT captured — federation is project-level, not operator-level. Spec MUST language: implementations MUST log emitted federated-patterns to the local pattern-aggregation-ledger BEFORE transmission.

entry_kind.description: Row-kind discriminator for `EmittedEntry`. Distinguishes from `ReceivedEntry` (which uses `entry_kind: "received"`).

ts.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at emit time (BEFORE transmission per Q12 log-before-transmit discipline). Monotonically increasing within a single ledger.

peer_brain_id.description: Opaque-hash identifier of the local Brain (the sender — i.e., the Brain that owns this ledger).

to_brain_id.description: Opaque-hash identifier of the receiving Brain (the peer the sender is transmitting to).

payload.description: The full federated-pattern payload as transmitted. Same cross-schema discipline as `ReceivedEntry.payload` — ledger validates STRUCTURE; federated-pattern schema validates CONTENT at the message-handler layer.
